root_text.an5.166:1.1: Tatra kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi …pe…
root_text.an5.166:1.2: “idhāvuso, bhikkhu sīlasampanno samādhisampanno paññāsampanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:1.4: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme aññaṁ ārādheyya, atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā udāyī āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.166:2.2: “aṭṭhānaṁ kho etaṁ, āvuso sāriputta, anavakāso yaṁ so bhikkhu atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:3.1: Dutiyampi kho …pe… tatiyampi kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an5.166:3.2: “idhāvuso, bhikkhu sīlasampanno samādhisampanno paññāsampanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:3.4: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme aññaṁ ārādheyya, atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:4.1: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā udāyī āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.166:4.2: “aṭṭhānaṁ kho etaṁ, āvuso sāriputta, anavakāso yaṁ so bhikkhu atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:5.2: “yāvatatiyakampi kho me āyasmā udāyī paṭikkosati, na ca me koci bhikkhu anumodati.
root_text.an5.166:5.4: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.an5.166:5.5: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an5.166:5.6: “idhāvuso, bhikkhu sīlasampanno samādhisampanno paññāsampanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:5.8: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme aññaṁ ārādheyya, atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:6.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā udāyī āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.166:6.2: “aṭṭhānaṁ kho etaṁ, āvuso sāriputta, anavakāso yaṁ so bhikkhu atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:7.1: Dutiyampi kho …pe… tatiyampi kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an5.166:7.2: “idhāvuso, bhikkhu sīlasampanno samādhisampanno paññāsampanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:7.4: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme aññaṁ ārādheyya, atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:8.1: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā udāyī āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.166:8.2: “aṭṭhānaṁ kho etaṁ, āvuso sāriputta, anavakāso yaṁ so bhikkhu atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:9.2: “bhagavatopi kho me sammukhā āyasmā udāyī yāvatatiyakaṁ paṭikkosati, na ca me koci bhikkhu anumodati.
root_text.an5.166:9.4: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.an5.166:10.1: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ āmantesi:
root_text.an5.166:10.2: “kaṁ pana tvaṁ, udāyi, manomayaṁ kāyaṁ paccesī”ti?
root_text.an5.166:10.4: “Kiṁ nu kho tuyhaṁ, udāyi, bālassa abyattassa bhaṇitena.
root_text.an5.166:11.1: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.an5.166:11.2: “atthi nāma, ānanda, theraṁ bhikkhuṁ vihesiyamānaṁ ajjhupekkhissatha.
root_text.an5.166:11.3: Na hi nāma, ānanda, kāruññampi bhavissati theramhi bhikkhumhi vihesiyamānamhī”ti.
root_text.an5.166:12.2: “idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sīlasampanno samādhisampanno paññāsampanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:12.4: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme aññaṁ ārādheyya, atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi—
root_text.an5.166:12.7: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ pāvisi.
root_text.an5.166:13.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando acirapakkantassa bhagavato yenāyasmā upavāṇo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upavāṇaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.166:13.4: Anacchariyaṁ kho, panetaṁ āvuso upavāṇa, yaṁ bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito etadeva ārabbha udāhareyya yathā āyasmantaṁyevettha upavāṇaṁ paṭibhāseyya.
root_text.an5.166:13.6: Atha kho bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yena upaṭṭhānasālā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. Nisajja kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ upavāṇaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.166:14.1: “Katihi nu kho, upavāṇa, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo cā”ti?
root_text.an5.166:14.2: “Pañcahi, bhante, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.166:14.4: Idha, bhante, thero bhikkhu sīlavā hoti …pe… samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu;
root_text.an5.166:14.6: kalyāṇavāco hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā;
root_text.an5.166:14.7: catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī;
root_text.an5.166:14.9: Imehi kho, bhante, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo cā”ti.
root_text.an5.166:15.2: Imehi kho, upavāṇa, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.166:15.3: Ime ce, upavāṇa, pañca dhammā therassa bhikkhuno na saṁvijjeyyuṁ, taṁ sabrahmacārī na sakkareyyuṁ na garuṁ kareyyuṁ na māneyyuṁ na pūjeyyuṁ khaṇḍiccena pāliccena valittacatāya.
root_text.an5.166:15.4: Yasmā ca kho, upavāṇa, ime pañca dhammā therassa bhikkhuno saṁvijjanti, tasmā taṁ sabrahmacārī sakkaronti garuṁ karonti mānenti pūjentī”ti.

translation_text.an5.166:1.1: There Venerable Sāriputta addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.an5.166:1.2: “Reverends, take a mendicant who is accomplished in ethics, immersion, and wisdom. They might enter into and emerge from the cessation of perception and feeling.
translation_text.an5.166:1.4: If they don’t reach enlightenment in this very life, then, surpassing the company of gods that consume edible food, they’re reborn in a certain host of mind-made gods. There they might enter into and emerge from the cessation of perception and feeling.
translation_text.an5.166:2.1: When he said this, Venerable Udāyī said to him,
translation_text.an5.166:2.2: “This is not possible, Reverend Sāriputta, it cannot happen!”
translation_text.an5.166:3.1: But for a second … and a third time Sāriputta repeated his statement.
translation_text.an5.166:4.1: And for a third time, Udāyī said to him,
translation_text.an5.166:4.2: “This is not possible, Reverend Sāriputta, it cannot happen!”
translation_text.an5.166:5.2: “Venerable Udāyī disagrees with me three times, and not one mendicant agrees with me.
translation_text.an5.166:5.3: Why don’t I go to see the Buddha?”
translation_text.an5.166:5.4: Then Sāriputta went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side,
translation_text.an5.166:5.6: “Reverends, take a mendicant who is accomplished in ethics, immersion, and wisdom. They might enter into and emerge from the cessation of perception and feeling.
translation_text.an5.166:5.8: If they don’t reach enlightenment in this very life, they’re reborn in the company of a certain host of mind-made gods, who surpass the gods that consume edible food. There they might enter into and emerge from the cessation of perception and feeling.
translation_text.an5.166:6.1: When he said this, Udāyī said to him,
translation_text.an5.166:6.2: “This is not possible, Reverend Sāriputta, it cannot happen!”
translation_text.an5.166:7.1: But for a second … and a third time Sāriputta repeated his statement.
translation_text.an5.166:8.1: And for a third time, Udāyī said to him,
translation_text.an5.166:8.2: “This is not possible, Reverend Sāriputta, it cannot happen!”
translation_text.an5.166:9.2: “Even in front of the Buddha Venerable Udāyī disagrees with me three times, and not one mendicant agrees with me.
translation_text.an5.166:10.1: Then the Buddha said to Venerable Udāyī,
translation_text.an5.166:10.2: “But Udāyī, do you believe in a mind-made body?”
translation_text.an5.166:10.3: “For those gods, sir, who are formless, made of perception.”
translation_text.an5.166:10.4: “Udāyī, what has an incompetent fool like you got to say?
translation_text.an5.166:10.5: How on earth could you imagine you’ve got something worth saying!”
translation_text.an5.166:11.1: Then the Buddha said to Venerable Ānanda,
translation_text.an5.166:11.2: “Ānanda! How can you just watch while a senior mendicant is being harassed?
translation_text.an5.166:11.3: Don’t you have any compassion for a senior mendicant who is being harassed?”
translation_text.an5.166:12.1: Then the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.an5.166:12.2: “Mendicants, take a mendicant who is accomplished in ethics, immersion, and wisdom. They might enter into and emerge from the cessation of perception and feeling.
translation_text.an5.166:12.4: If they don’t reach enlightenment in this very life, they’re reborn in the company of a certain host of mind-made gods, who surpass the gods that consume edible food. There they might enter into and emerge from the cessation of perception and feeling.
translation_text.an5.166:12.6: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.an5.166:12.7: When he had spoken, the Holy One got up from his seat and entered his dwelling.
translation_text.an5.166:13.1: Then, not long after the Buddha had left, Venerable Ānanda went to Venerable Upavāṇa and said to him,
translation_text.an5.166:13.2: “Reverend Upavāṇa, they’ve been harassing other senior mendicants,
translation_text.an5.166:13.4: I wouldn’t be surprised if the Buddha brings this up when he comes out of retreat later this afternoon. He might even call upon Venerable Upavāṇa himself.
translation_text.an5.166:13.5: And right now I feel timid.”
translation_text.an5.166:13.6: Then in the late afternoon, the Buddha came out of retreat and went to the assembly hall, where he sat on the seat spread out, and said to Upavāṇa,
translation_text.an5.166:14.1: “Upavāṇa, how many qualities should a senior mendicant have to be dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired?”
translation_text.an5.166:14.2: “Sir, a senior mendicant with five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.
translation_text.an5.166:14.4: It’s when a mendicant is ethical, restrained in the monastic code, conducting themselves well and resorting for alms in suitable places. Seeing danger in the slightest fault, they keep the rules they’ve undertaken.
translation_text.an5.166:14.5: They’re very learned, remembering and keeping what they’ve learned. These teachings are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased, describing a spiritual practice that’s totally full and pure. They are very learned in such teachings, remembering them, reciting them, mentally scrutinizing them, and penetrating them theoretically.
translation_text.an5.166:14.6: They’re a good speaker who enunciates well, with a polished, clear, and articulate voice that expresses the meaning.
translation_text.an5.166:14.7: They get the four absorptions—blissful meditations in this life that belong to the higher mind—when they want, without trouble or difficulty.
translation_text.an5.166:14.8: They realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an5.166:14.9: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.”
translation_text.an5.166:15.2: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.
translation_text.an5.166:15.3: If these five qualities are not found in a senior mendicant, why would their spiritual companions honor, respect, revere, or venerate them? Because of their broken teeth, gray hair, and wrinkled skin?
translation_text.an5.166:15.4: But since these five qualities are found in a senior mendicant, their spiritual companions honor, respect, revere, or venerate them.”

variant_text.an5.166:1.2: samāpajjeyyāpi vuṭṭhaheyyāpi → samāpajjeyyapi vuṭṭhaheyyapi (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an5.166:1.4: kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ → kabaḷiṅkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ (bj, cck, km, pts1ed); kabaliṁ kārāhārabhakkhānaṁ (sya1ed, sya2ed)
